description_long: This is a rustic, public water point featuring a stone basin and a vertical wooden structure with a pitched roof. The basin is rectangular, made of weathered stone, and sits atop a cobblestone platform, showing signs of moss and age. The vertical structure houses a metal spout protruding from its center, with a small overhang to protect the spout area. There is a visible round metal plate on the side of the wooden structure, possibly a historical marker or label. The water point is surrounded by a stone curb, and the cobblestone base extends around the entire object, providing a stable footing. The overall appearance suggests it is a traditional or historical urban amenity, likely found in European towns or parks.
description: A traditional stone and wood water point with a pitched roof, metal spout, and cobblestone base, showing signs of age and public use.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the tall wooden structure with a pitched roof and the back of the stone basin.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the round metal label on the wooden structure and the profile of the stone basin.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the metal spout, the open stone basin, and the main area for water collection.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the open framework of the wooden structure and the side of the basin.
attribute_spout_type: straight metal pipe spout, horizontally oriented, protruding from the wooden structure
attribute_mounting_location: floor-standing, mounted on a cobblestone platform with stone curb
attribute_signage_or_labeling: round metal label with text on the side of the wooden structure
